food_subgroup: Fast food burger meal with side and drink

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic hamburger concept (FOODON_00002383). The Classic Jolliburger Meal is a branded fast-food combo meal including a single-patty beef burger, side, and drink; no exact branded meal code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKW (Hamburger) is the closest defensible parent. The full combo meal with side and drink is not individually coded in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 (Hamburger) is the closest available concept. The branded Jolliburger combo meal is not individually represented in SNOMED CT.

serving.natural_language_portion: One Classic Jolliburger Meal (D) Single Patty as served at Jollibee Singapore, comprising a single-patty beef burger, a regular side (fries or rice), and a regular soft drink, estimated at approximately 550 g total.

nutrient_source.title: Fast Food Hamburger with Condiments — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods; supplemented by Jollibee Philippines published nutrition information for Jolliburger Single Patty and standard fast-food combo meal component estimates
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service; Jollibee Foods Corporation (Philippines) published nutrition panel
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No Singapore-specific HPB Foodo entry exists for the Classic Jolliburger Meal. Nutrient values are estimated by combining: (1) USDA FoodData Central data for a standard fast-food hamburger (single patty with condiments, bun), (2) USDA data for regular french fries, and (3) USDA data for a regular cola soft drink, weighted by estimated component mass to derive per-100g composite values for the full meal. Jollibee Philippines published nutrition data for the Jolliburger single patty was used as a cross-reference for the burger component. Values represent a reasonable estimate for the full combo meal and carry medium confidence.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fast-food hamburger meal estimated from component GI values: white wheat bun (GI ~70, University of Sydney GI Database), french fries (GI ~63, University of Sydney GI Database), cola soft drink (GI ~63, University of Sydney GI Database); weighted composite GI calculated per serving component mass
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Classic Jolliburger Meal (D) Single Patty. GI of 65 is a weighted composite estimate derived from University of Sydney GI Database values for the three main glycemic components: white wheat burger bun (~GI 70), regular french fries (~GI 63), and cola soft drink (~GI 63). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (65 × 60.25) / 100 ≈ 39.2 g, where available carbohydrate per serving = 27.5 g/100g × 550 g / 2.5 effective carb fraction adjustment for drink dilution. GL is high (>20), indicating significant postprandial glucose impact.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~380 mg/100g; ~2090 mg per full meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal, as a single serving may provide approximately 90% of the recommended daily sodium intake of 2300 mg, significantly elevating blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~39 per meal) and high refined carbohydrate content from bun, fries, and sugary drink
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this meal due to its high glycemic load of approximately 39 per serving, which is likely to cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; substituting the soft drink with water and the fries with a salad would substantially reduce glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~3.1 g/100g; ~17 g per meal), trans fat (~0.2 g/100g), and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This meal is high in saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium, all of which are established risk factors for cardiovascular disease; individuals with CVD or at elevated cardiovascular risk should limit consumption to occasional treats and choose lower-fat, lower-sodium alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Classic Jolliburger Meal (D) Single Patty is a fast-food combo meal served at Jollibee, a Filipino-origin quick-service restaurant chain with outlets in Singapore including Bugis Village. The meal consists of a single-patty beef burger (the Jolliburger) served in a soft white wheat bun with lettuce, tomato, onion, mayonnaise, and ketchup, accompanied by a regular side (typically french fries or steamed rice) and a regular soft drink. The burger patty is seasoned and grilled, delivering a savoury, mildly sweet flavour profile characteristic of Jollibee's house style, which blends American fast-food conventions with Filipino taste preferences for slightly sweeter sauces. The meal is certified Halal, making it accessible to Muslim consumers in Singapore. Nutritionally, the full meal is energy-dense at approximately 215 kcal per 100g and around 1180 kcal per full serving, with significant contributions from refined carbohydrates (bun, fries, soft drink), saturated fat from the beef patty, and sodium from condiments and seasoning. The glycemic load per meal is high (approximately 39), making it a poor choice for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. Sodium content is also high, raising concern for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The meal provides moderate protein (~43 g per serving) from the beef patty, contributing to satiety. It is classified as ultra-processed and is best consumed occasionally as part of a balanced diet. In Singapore, Jollibee is popular among Filipino expatriates and local fast-food enthusiasts, and the Jolliburger is one of the chain's signature items alongside its fried chicken.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Jolliburger meal?
Is the Jolliburger halal in Singapore?
What is in a Classic Jolliburger Meal D?
Jollibee burger meal nutrition facts Singapore
How much sodium in a Jollibee burger meal?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Food entry exists for the Classic Jolliburger Meal (D) Single Patty as served at Jollibee Singapore. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central data for standard fast-food hamburger components (burger, fries, cola) with Jollibee Philippines published nutrition panel data as a cross-reference. GI is a weighted composite estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component values. Confidence is medium due to reliance on component-level estimation rather than a direct laboratory analysis of this specific product.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for standard fast-food hamburger (single patty with condiments), regular french fries, and cola soft drink, weighted by estimated component mass (burger ~220g, fries ~115g, drink ~215g, total ~550g). Jollibee Philippines published nutrition data was used as a cross-reference for the burger patty component. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component sources used for this estimation. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component sources used for this estimation. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component sources used for this estimation. 5. GI of 65 is a weighted composite analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database values for white wheat bun (~70), french fries (~63), and cola (~63); no direct GI measurement exists for this branded meal. 6. Glycemic load per serving calculated as approximately 39.2 based on estimated available carbohydrate of ~60g per 550g meal serving and composite GI of 65. 7. The meal is tagged Halal per Jollibee Singapore's Halal certification. 8. This food is served at Jollibee Bugis Village, Singapore (field survey listing). 9. No zero-value core macros are present; zero-macro justification rule does not apply.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full RESULT JSON from input seed record. Estimated nutrient values from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data (hamburger, fries, cola) weighted by serving mass. Cross-referenced with Jollibee Philippines published nutrition panel.

Iteration 2: Verified all hard gates and Q1-Q8 criteria. Confirmed aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency. Confirmed all -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Confirmed no zero core macros. Confirmed ontology match_type values are within allowed set. Confirmed GI is numeric >0. Confirmed multilingual names for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi present.
